summary.summary: TelemetryClient.buildPayload was changed to compute appleHealthEnabled from per-type HealthKit write permissions instead of a single "full write" boolean. The change adds an import of HealthKit, builds a list of the five Trio-managed sample types (glucose, insulin, carbs, fat, protein), queries healthKitManager.checkWriteToHealthPermissions for each type, sets payload["appleHealthEnabled"] = true if any per-type permission is granted, and includes a payload["appleHealthWrites"] map with per-type booleans when available.
summary.userFacingImpact: Telemetry payload semantics changed: appleHealthEnabled will be reported true if any of the five Trio-managed sample types (glucose, insulin, carbs, fat, protein) has write permission, and a new appleHealthWrites object provides the per-type boolean snapshot. According to the PR description this addresses a reporter's case where appleHealthEnabled was false despite a working Apple Health integration. Downstream consumers of telemetry may observe different semantics compared to the previous behavior that required all five types to be authorized.
